disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Repeated stiffening (tonic) episodes of 2-3 minutes each, back to back, without the child fully regaining consciousness between them, ongoing for about 30 minutes
trigger_question_examples: How long has this been going on and is he waking up between the fits?
How long does each stiffening episode last?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Each stiffening lasts about two or three minutes and they keep coming one after another. It's been going for half an hour and he hasn't properly woken up in between.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden loss of all muscle tone (drop attack) causing a fall and blunt facial trauma, in a child with a known severe epilepsy
trigger_question_examples: What happened right at the start?
How did he hurt his face?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He just went completely floppy out of nowhere and dropped, and his face hit the table. Then the stiffening fits started.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Active bleeding from a deep laceration above the left eyebrow following the fall
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the cut on his face?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a deep cut above his left eyebrow, about 3 cm, and it's still bleeding.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Post-ictal obtundation — profoundly drowsy, floppy, not visually tracking, only stirs to touch
trigger_question_examples: How is he responding right now?
Is he awake and looking at you between the episodes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's really drowsy and floppy now, not looking at us, and only moves a bit when we touch him.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Child has a known severe epilepsy (Lennox-Gastaut) with multiple refractory seizure types including drop attacks, on several daily antiepileptic medicines
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions?
Does he have a history of seizures?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, he has a severe epilepsy — Lennox-Gastaut Syndrome — and lots of different seizure types. He takes valproate, lamotrigine and clobazam every day.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Missed morning dose of clobazam today
trigger_question_examples: Has he had all his usual medicines today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you ask — he missed his morning clobazam today, we got behind with the routine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Child is afebrile and there is no preceding illness or fever
trigger_question_examples: Has he had a fever or been unwell recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he hasn't had a fever or been sick — he doesn't feel hot at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No working rescue medication has been given yet during this cluster
trigger_question_examples: Does he have an emergency rescue medicine for seizures?
Have you given him anything to stop the seizures?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has an emergency medicine for his cheek that the neurologist prescribed, but in the panic we haven't given it yet.
